title: A Divided Fed Holds Rates Steady
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady in a highly divided decision, with rising inflation concerns and three dissenting officials. Powell indicated he
excerpt: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady in a highly divided decision, with rising inflation concerns and three dissenting officials. Powell indicated he would remain on the Board of Governors. Markets expect no changes for the rest of the year and into 2027.

content.introduction: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady in a highly divided decision, with rising inflation concerns and three dissenting officials. Powell indicated he would remain on the Board of Governors. Markets expect no changes for the rest of the year and into 2027.
body: An unusually divided [Federal Reserve](https://www.cnbc.com/federal-reserve/) on Wednesday held its key interest rate steady as policymakers grappled with the policy impact of persistent inflation and awaited [a looming leadership transition](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/29/trump-fed-nominee-kevin-warsh-senate-approval.html) at the central bank. In what may have been Chair [Jerome Powell](https://www.cnbc.com/jay-powell/)'s final meeting at the helm, the rate-setting Federal Open Market Committee voted to hold the benchmark funds rate in a range between 3.5%-3.75%. Separately, during a news conference following the central bank's decision, Powell signaled that he would [remain on the Board of Governors for an indefinite period](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/29/jerome-powell-says-he-will-continue-to-serve-as-a-fed-governor-even-after-chairmanship-ends-.html). Markets had been widely expecting the hold and in fact are [pricing in no changes the rest of this year](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/28/fed-meeting-preview-april-2026.html) and well into 2027. Fed officials at the [March meeting](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/fed-meeting-today-live-updates.html) indicated they foresee one cut this year then another in 2027, putting the funds rate down to its expected "neutral" level around 3.1%. [Powell has spoken](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/29/fed-meeting-today-live-updates-warsh-powell.html) strongly about Fed independence.
An unusually divided Federal Reserve on Wednesday held its key interest rate steady as policymakers grappled with the policy impact of

*   [Federal Reserve](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/29/business/economy/takeaways-from-powells-final-fed-meeting-as-chair.html). *   [Trump’s Fed Pick](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/29/business/economy/warsh-federal-reserve-chair.html). ## [Takeaways from Powell’s final Fed meeting as chair.](https://www.nytimes.com/live/2026/04/29/business/federal-reserve-interest-rates#takeaways-from-powells-final-news-conference-as-fed-chair). ## [Powell says he will stay on as Fed governor after his term as chair ends.](https://www.nytimes.com/live/2026/04/29/business/federal-reserve-interest-rates#powell-fed-stay-governor). Powell said on Wednesday that he would stay on as a governor at the Federal Reserve after his term as chair ends May 15,citing lingering [legal threats](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/24/us/politics/federal-reserve-powell-pirro-investigation.html) against him and the central bank as part of a pressure campaign by President Trump for lower borrowing costs. For Mr. Powell, the Justice Department’s [investigation](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/16/us/politics/powell-trump-investigation.html) was just another attempt to browbeat the Fed into lowering interest rates, which Mr. Trump has long demanded the central bank deliver. Mr. Tillis [lifted](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/26/business/economy/tillis-federal-reserve-nomination.html) his blockade on Sunday, saying he had received assurances from federal prosecutors that the legal threats against Mr. Powell had ended. Mr. Powell’s decision to stay on will set up another clash with the president, who has [vowed](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/15/business/trump-powell-fed-fire.html) to fire him if he does not resign from the Fed when his term as chair ends.
